analysis.summary: TiDB is a distributed SQL database designed for horizontal scalability, high availability, and hybrid transactional/analytical processing (HTAP). It implements a three-tier architecture with TiDB server nodes handling SQL processing, TiKV providing distributed transactional storage using Raft consensus, and TiFlash offering columnar storage for analytics. The system supports MySQL 8.0 protocol compatibility and provides strong ACID guarantees across distributed transactions.
capabilities: Provides distributed SQL query processing with MySQL 8.0 protocol compatibility (cmd/tidb-server/main.go, pkg/server/)
Implements distributed transactional storage with Raft consensus protocol for strong consistency (pkg/store/, TiKV integration)
Supports backup and restore operations for cluster data (br/cmd/br/main.go, br/pkg/backup/, br/pkg/restore/)
Enables data import/export through Lightning fast parallel data loading (lightning/cmd/, dumpling/cmd/dumpling/main.go)
Manages distributed DDL operations across cluster nodes (pkg/ddl/)
Provides columnar analytics storage engine integration with TiFlash (pkg/store/)
Handles session management and connection pooling (pkg/session/, pkg/sessionctx/)
Implements SQL query planning and optimization (pkg/planner/)
Supports distributed SQL execution with MPP processing (pkg/executor/, pkg/executor/mppcoordmanager/)
Manages privilege and access control with RBAC (pkg/privilege/)
Provides statistics collection and analysis for query optimization (pkg/statistics/)
Handles distributed lock management and deadlock detection (pkg/lock/, pkg/util/deadlockhistory/)
Supports plugin architecture for extensibility (pkg/plugin/, pkg/extension/)
Implements resource group management and workload isolation (pkg/resourcegroup/, pkg/resourcemanager/)
Provides telemetry and monitoring metrics collection (pkg/telemetry/, pkg/metrics/)
Handles table partitioning and sharding logic (pkg/table/, pkg/tablecodec/)
Manages keyspace isolation for multi-tenancy (pkg/keyspace/)
Supports time-to-live (TTL) data management (pkg/ttl/)
Provides expression evaluation and type system (pkg/expression/, pkg/types/)
Implements auto-increment ID generation service (pkg/autoid_service/)
Handles domain and schema management (pkg/domain/, pkg/infoschema/)
Supports bind variables and SQL plan binding (pkg/bindinfo/)
Provides distributed garbage collection management (br/pkg/gc/)
Implements streaming change data capture (br/pkg/stream/)
Supports external storage backends (S3, GCS, Azure) for backup/restore (br/pkg/aws/, cloud storage integrations)
use_cases: Database administrators use TiDB to deploy horizontally scalable MySQL-compatible databases for high-traffic applications
Data engineers use the backup/restore tools to manage cluster data migration and disaster recovery
Application developers use TiDB as a drop-in replacement for MySQL with automatic horizontal scaling
Analytics teams use TiFlash integration for real-time OLAP queries on transactional data
DevOps teams use Lightning for fast bulk data loading and Dumpling for data export operations
analysis.target_audience: Database administrators, Backend developers, Data engineers, DevOps engineers
technical_stack: TiKV distributed key-value store (tikv/client-go/v2 in go.mod)
gRPC for inter-service communication (google.golang.org/grpc in go.mod)
Apache Arrow for columnar data processing (github.com/apache/arrow-go/v18 in go.mod)
AWS SDK for cloud storage (aws/aws-sdk-go-v2 in go.mod)
api_endpoints: MySQL protocol on port 4000 (default TiDB server port from Dockerfile EXPOSE)
HTTP status endpoint for health checks (pkg/server/)
Lightning web UI endpoints for import monitoring (lightning/web/)
gRPC endpoints for internal cluster communication (pkg/server/)
sdk_languages: Any language with MySQL connector support
integrations: TiFlash for columnar analytics (pkg/store/ TiFlash integration)
Placement Driver (PD) for cluster coordination (github.com/tikv/pd imports)

components: TiDB Server - stateless SQL processing layer
Placement Driver (PD) - cluster metadata and scheduling
architecture.data_flow: SQL queries enter through TiDB server nodes, get parsed and planned, then distributed to TiKV nodes for transactional processing or TiFlash for analytical processing. Data is automatically sharded across TiKV regions using Raft consensus for consistency. Results are aggregated back through TiDB nodes to clients.
architecture.persistence: TiKV provides persistent distributed storage with Raft replication, TiFlash maintains columnar replicas, automatic schema versioning and DDL coordination
architecture.concurrency_model: async with goroutines for high concurrency SQL processing, Raft-based distributed consensus for data consistency
architecture.communication_patterns: gRPC for internal cluster communication, MySQL protocol for client connections, HTTP for monitoring and admin APIs
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Plugin system in pkg/plugin/ and pkg/extension/ for custom functionality, hooks for DDL operations, extensible storage backends

storage_backends: External storage (S3, GCS, Azure) for backups
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational SQL schema with MySQL compatibility, distributed sharding by key ranges, multi-version concurrency control (MVCC)
data_model.migration_strategy: Online DDL operations coordinated across cluster, schema versioning in pkg/domain/, backward compatibility maintenance

algorithms.problem_type: Distributed SQL processing with HTAP workloads requiring horizontal scaling and strong consistency
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Handles thousands of nodes, millions of QPS, automatic load balancing and failover
algorithms.evidence: pkg/ddl/ for distributed DDL, pkg/planner/ for query optimization, tikv integration for Raft consensus, pkg/executor/ for distributed execution

security_model.authentication: MySQL native auth, SSL/TLS certificates, cluster internal authentication
security_model.authorization: Role-based access control (RBAC) with fine-grained privileges (pkg/privilege/)
security_model.data_protection: TLS encryption in transit, encryption at rest in TiKV, certificate-based cluster auth
security_model.security_headers: Configurable CORS support, secure client connections
security_model.vulnerability_handling: SECURITY.md policy, dependency scanning, security patches

deployment.container_orchestration: Docker Compose / Kubernetes with TiDB Operator
deployment.ci_cd: Custom Prow-based CI system (prow.tidb.net from README badges)
deployment.environment_config: Configuration files, environment variables, command-line flags, TOML config format
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal auto-scaling by adding TiDB/TiKV/TiFlash nodes, automatic data rebalancing

code_quality_signals.code_style: Standard Go formatting with gofmt, extensive code review process

project_health.recent_activity: Active development with import size estimation features
project_health.community_signals: Code of conduct, security policy, contribution guidelines, issue templates

information_gaps: Full API endpoint details would require deeper source analysis
Performance benchmarks not visible in provided extraction

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: TiDB orchestrates distributed SQL processing across multiple storage engines (TiKV, TiFlash), coordinates distributed transactions using two-phase commit, manages cluster-wide DDL operations, and handles automatic data rebalancing and failover. It maintains complex cluster state including placement rules, region mappings, and schema versioning.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: MySQL 8.0 protocol compatibility, strict SQL schema enforcement, relational data model with ACID guarantees, typed expression system in pkg/types/, and structured DDL operations coordinated across cluster nodes.
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Uses Raft consensus protocol in TiKV for replication, implements Percolator-style distributed transactions with two-phase commit, employs range-based sharding with automatic splitting/merging, and coordinates distributed DDL operations with schema versioning across cluster nodes.
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Implements distributed transactions with strong consistency guarantees, uses Raft consensus for data replication, provides MVCC for concurrent access, includes distributed lock management and deadlock detection in pkg/lock/, and maintains ACID properties across distributed storage.
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Coordinates distributed execution of structured queries across multiple heterogeneous storage engines while maintaining strong consistency guarantees and providing transparent horizontal scaling with automatic data distribution and rebalancing.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Query ingestion → parsing/planning → distributed execution coordination → heterogeneous storage access → result aggregation → response delivery, with background processes for data rebalancing, schema coordination, and consistency maintenance
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The core distributed coordination patterns, consensus algorithms, and multi-engine orchestration logic are highly reusable across domains requiring distributed consistency. The SQL parsing and MySQL protocol layers are domain-specific, but the underlying distributed state management, automatic sharding, and multi-storage coordination patterns could be abstracted for other structured data systems. The plugin architecture and resource management components provide good extension points.

evidence_claims.claim: Implements distributed consensus for strong consistency
evidence_claims.evidence: TiKV integration with Raft consensus protocol, distributed transaction support in pkg/store/
TiKV for transactional storage and TiFlash for columnar analytics in pkg/store/, different storage backends

evidence_claims.claim: Provides automatic horizontal scaling and data rebalancing
evidence_claims.evidence: Range-based sharding, region splitting/merging, placement rules in pkg/tablecodec/ and architecture description
Plugin system in pkg/plugin/ and pkg/extension/ with hooks for custom functionality
Distributed DDL management in pkg/ddl/ with online schema changes and versioning

cross_industry_applications: Financial Trading Systems - distributed order matching with ACID guarantees across multiple exchanges
Supply Chain Management - coordinated inventory tracking across multiple warehouses with consistency requirements
Telecommunications Billing - distributed call detail record processing with real-time analytics and strict consistency
Gaming Platforms - distributed player state management across multiple game servers with leaderboard analytics
IoT Data Platforms - coordinated sensor data ingestion with real-time analytics across edge and cloud storage
Healthcare Records - distributed patient data management with HIPAA compliance and cross-facility coordination
Content Management - distributed media asset coordination across CDN nodes with metadata consistency
E-commerce Platforms - distributed inventory and order management with real-time reporting across multiple channels

friction_report.migration_path: Extract the distributed coordination layer and consensus algorithms, replace SQL-specific components with domain-appropriate query/command processors, adapt the multi-storage coordination patterns for target storage systems, and customize the plugin system for domain-specific extensions.

output.format: SQL result sets or execution status
output.delivery: sync for queries, async for DDL operations

authentication.method: username/password with SSL/TLS encryption and certificate-based cluster auth

comparable_systems: Apache Kafka with Schema Registry - distributed event coordination with multiple storage tiers and schema evolution
Kubernetes Control Plane - distributed resource orchestration across heterogeneous compute nodes with etcd consensus
Apache Spark Cluster Manager - coordinated distributed computation across multiple storage systems with fault tolerance
Elasticsearch Cluster - distributed search coordination across multiple node types with automatic rebalancing
Redis Cluster with Sentinel - distributed cache coordination with automatic failover and data distribution
information_gaps: Detailed placement rule algorithms and data distribution strategies
Specific consensus protocol optimizations beyond standard Raft
Resource isolation mechanisms and multi-tenancy implementation details
Backup/restore coordination protocols and consistency guarantees
